memoryArtifacts.headline: James Savage, "A Genealogical Dictionary," v. 4, page 644
memoryArtifacts.description: James Savage, "A Genealogical Dictionary," v. 4, page 644

Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Woodward, Woodard, Woodwards, Woodyard, Wadard and many more. First found in Essex where Commander Wadard was granted lands (Domesday Book - 1086) by King William for his assistance at the Battle of Hastings. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Christopher Woodward settled in Virginia in 1620; Henry and Mary Woodward settled in Virginia in 1623; along with Richard; John Woodward settled in Boston, Mass. in 1634. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009) Motto Translated: Virtue is always flourishing. This interesting surname is of Anglo-Saxon origin, and comes from a medieval occupational name for a forester (which has formed another well known surname), a man employed to look after the trees and the game in the forest. This was a very important and well respected profession, since the woods and the game animals therein were usually preserved against poachers and were solely for the use and enjoyment of the king or the local lord of the manor. The "New Forest" in Hampshire is a prime example of such a protected area and the punishment for infringement was usually death. The derivation of the name is from the Old English pre-7th Century "wudu", wood, and "weard" guardian or protector. One Christopher Woodward was an early settler in Virginia, leaving London in the "Tryall" in June 1620 for America. A Coat of Arms granted to the Woodward family of Woodmarsh, Kent, depicts a black chevron between three green grasshoppers on a silver shield. A demi woman couped at the knees, clad in red garments and with her gold hair disheveled, holding in her right hand a honeysuckle, is on the Crest. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Sewhal le Wudeward, which was dated 1208, in the "Pipe Rolls of Hampshire", during the reign of King John, known as "Lackland", 1199 - 1216. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward於大約1610年出生在英國。他於1639年9月4日與Elizabeth Mather在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1683年4月7日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓享年74歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward于大约1610年出生在英国。他于1639年9月4日与Elizabeth Mather在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1683年4月7日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓享年74岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Woodward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a forester employed to look after the trees and game in a forest, Middle English woodward (from Old English wudu ‘wood’ + weard ‘guardian, protector’).

History: Henry Woodward emigrated from England in 1635 and settled first in Dorchester, MA, and subsequently in Northampton, MA. He had many prominent descendants. Another Henry Woodward, born c. 1646 in the British West Indies, was the first English settler in SC (1664).

variants.name: Woodard
meanings: from the Norman Old French personal name Odard, Udard, Middle English Wodard, Wudard. It derives from the ancient Germanic name Authard, Othard, Odard, composed of the elements aud- ‘riches' + hard- ‘hard, bold’.
occupational name from Middle English wode ‘wood’ + herde ‘herd’ (Old English wudu + hierde), for a person who tended animals pastured in woodland.

variants.name: Woodworth
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Woodward or Wadworth, the latter being a habitational name from Wadworth in Yorkshire, which is derived from the Old English personal name Wada, Wadda + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Woodman
meanings: occupational name for a woodcutter or a forester (compare Woodward ), from Middle English wodeman ‘woodman’. In some instances this may be a variant of Wadman .
from the Middle English personal name Wudemann (Old English Wuduman, from wudu ‘wood’ + mann ‘man’).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward was born about 1610, in England. He married Elizabeth Mather on 4 September 1639, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 7 April 1683, in Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 74, and was buried in Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward est né environ 1610, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Elizabeth Mather le 4 septembre 1639, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 7 avril 1683, à Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward è nato il circa 1610 nel England. Lui sposato Elizabeth Mather il 4 settembre 1639, nel Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 7 aprile 1683, nel Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.Dorchester, Boston, Suffolk, Massachuset: アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンドールチェスター
ja.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodwardは約1610年にイギリスで生まれた。1639年9月4日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターでElizabeth Matherと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1683年4月7日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアノーサンプトンで74歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアノーサンプトンにて埋葬された。

ko.Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 도르체스터
ko.Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터
ko.Northampton, Massachusetts Bay Colony, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르섬프턴
ko.Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Ba: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 노르섬프턴
ko.Came to United States on Ship James in t: 미국 인디애나 주 라그랑 리마 타운시프 온타리오
ko.Dorchester, Boston, Suffolk, Massachuset: 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 도르체스터
ko.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward(은)는 대략 1610년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1639년 9월 4일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 Elizabeth Mather(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1683년 4월 7일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 노르섬프턴에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 노르섬프턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward nasceu aproximadamente 1610, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Mather em 4 de setembro de 1639, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de abril de 1683, em Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward родился около 1610 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Mather 4 сентября 1639 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 7 апреля 1683 г. в местности нортамптон, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности нортамптон, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Dr. Henry Woodward nació aproximadamente 1610, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Elizabeth Mather el 4 de septiembre de 1639, en Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 7 de abril de 1683, en Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
